title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Michelle Davidson, 33, from Everett, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with the Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation.
Davidson's inspiration comes from the 2013 Boston Marathon bombings and the community's response to the tragedy.
The Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation supports trauma care, honoring the teams who responded to the 2013 bombings.

title: Boston faces budget deficit of nearly $50 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston faces a $48.4 million budget deficit due to costs in snow removal, police overtime, and health insurance.
City officials have paused hiring and frozen some spending to address the deficit, aiming for closure by June 30.
Boston Public Schools is managing a separate $53 million deficit, potentially cutting 400 positions despite a partial hiring freeze.

title: Squirrels, spinach doughnuts, and sirens: New England’s best April Fools’ jokes
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: New England's April Fools' Day included a joke about spinach doughnuts, surprising many with the unusual flavor.
One prank involved squirrels becoming traffic enforcers, creating a humorous reaction among local residents.
A city implemented fake emergency sirens to catch April Fools' Day participants off guard.

title: Where do the ‘Real Housewives of Rhode Island’ live?
author: Cameron Sperance
bullets: 'Real Housewives of Rhode Island' debuts April 2 on Bravo, featuring homes in Providence, Cranston, and Newport.
Cranston is highlighted for its residential architecture mix and accessible price points in the reality show.
East Greenwich, Newport, and Providence are the main filming locations, showcasing luxury and competitive real estate.

title: She’s running Boston for the hospital that saved her daughter’s life
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Leslie Wallus, 44, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital.
Wallus's daughter, Eloise, survived a brain tumor with treatment at Massachusetts General Hospital.
She's raising support for MGH's pediatric oncology unit, inspired by her daughter's recovery.

title: She’s running Boston to support people with visual impairments
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Fransisca Miraningtyas, 46, from Jakarta, Indonesia, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Massachusetts Association for the Blind and Visually Impaired.
She aims to support individuals with visual impairments, inspired by her own experiences with a vision condition called pterygium.
Miraningtyas's run symbolizes hope for those needing eye surgery, highlighting the financial barriers many face in accessing treatment.

title: Westfield man busted in alleged Red Bull SNAP scam at Chicopee smoke shop
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mohamed Yahiya, 47, of Westfield, charged with fraudulent SNAP/EBT transactions at Adham's Smoke Shop in Chicopee.
Yahiya allegedly bought Red Bull back for cash from individuals using SNAP benefits, says Chicopee Police.
Charges include food stamp trafficking, possession of unstamped cigarettes, and intent to distribute a Class D drug.

title: Free speech rally planned outside BU president’s office amid Pride flag dispute
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A free speech rally is scheduled for Thursday at Boston University after Pride flags were removed under a signage policy.
The rally, organized by Terrier Courage, seeks to challenge BU's policy limiting signage on outward-facing windows.
A petition with over 1,800 signatures urges BU to revise its policy to allow signage in personal spaces, supporting free expression.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in April
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: HBO Max adds the final season of 'Hacks' on April 9 and 'Euphoria' on April 13.
Prime Video's new comedy film 'Balls Up,' featuring Mark Wahlberg, streams starting April 15.
Apple TV releases 'Widow’s Bay,' filmed in Massachusetts, starring Matthew Rhys, on April 29.

title: What to know about the MBTA’s free and discounted commuter rail plans for summer
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MBTA offers free commuter rail rides on Fridays from June to August, excluding special event trains like the CapeFLYER.
Monthly commuter rail passes are discounted by 50% for Zones 1 through 10 during June, July, and August.
Passholders can travel to any zone on weekends and bring a companion for $1, excluding certain special event trains.
